body: Good catch. I pulled the enumerator sheet and you're right, it reads Hollis. Go ahead and open a PR against the Ward 3 rows.
Thanks Sam. PR is up as #153 with the sheet cited.
The Maryland State Archives parish register confirms the 1848 date. I'll add the citation with the volume and page.
Confirmed the duplicate. Keep the entry with the sexton's map plot number and drop the other.
Age 74 doesn't fit the household. The original clearly reads 47, the digits were transposed. Fixing.
I have the roll numbers from my last archives visit. Will attach them to the 1880 references.
The 1871 marriage license settles it. Please correct the transcribed year to match the license index.
Agreed on spelling Cornelius out in full and keeping the abbreviation in the source note. I'll do a pass.
1855 conflicts with her census age. The parish register says 1885, so let's go with that.
I have the 1890 city directory scan with page numbers. I'll add the citation to the affected rows.
The marker is very worn. Let's hold this until a second reader compares it with the rubbing photo.
Confirmed 'Anne Arundal' appears in a dozen rows. Straight spelling fix, I'll double-check nothing legitimately uses that spelling.
The sheet reads grandchild. Juan, can you prepare the correction PR when you have a moment?
Will do. I'll cite the enumerator sheet in the PR description.
A legend for the occupation abbreviations would help a lot. I can draft one from the standard census key.
The cemetery register gives March 4 1902. I'll fill in the month and day and cite the register.
Photo references would be great. Please add the photo IDs so anyone can verify the readings.
Dwellings 118 and 119 are clearly separate on the sheet. I've split the merged row.
ISO dates make sense. Keep the original wording in a source note so we don't lose the primary text.
PR is up adding the parish register citation. Thanks for the quick review, Sam.
Happy to write the worked illustration for the marriage license index. I'll base it on the Ridout-Hollis citation.
Right, '8/12' means eight months, not eight years. Good spot. Please correct per the census convention.
1999 can't be right for someone born in the 1820s. It's a typo for 1899. Fixing the year.
Reviewed the grandchild correction PR. Looks good, merging.
Dwelling 45 is on the sheet between 44 and 46. I'll transcribe the household and insert it.
Roll numbers added and everything checks out against the film. Merged, thanks Juan.
